L1.area: BSON document parsing and data type conversion logic

L2.description: A remote code execution vulnerability exists where user-supplied input is evaluated using an unsafe evaluation mechanism within a sandboxed context. An authenticated attacker can craft input that escapes the sandbox and executes arbitrary operating system commands on the host machine.

L3.area: BSON string-to-document parsing and type coercion utilities
L3.description: A remote code execution vulnerability exists where user-supplied input from database management form fields is evaluated using an unsafe evaluation mechanism within a sandboxed context. An authenticated attacker can craft input that escapes the sandbox via prototype chain traversal and executes arbitrary operating system commands on the host machine.

ground_truth.reason: The toBSON function passes user-controlled strings directly into eval() within a VM sandbox, allowing authenticated users to escape the sandbox via prototype chain traversal and execute arbitrary OS commands on the host.
